data.description: Blow up features.ts (currently ~300 lines, does terrain/imagery/contours/labels) into a features/ directory — one file per layer. Implement a layer registry pattern where each layer self-registers with a common interface (id, name, init, show, hide, destroy). Slim AppState to { exaggeration: 1|EXAGGERATION_SCALE, layers: Record<LayerId, boolean> }. Renderer becomes a thin orchestrator. UI holds zero rendering knowledge. This is the foundational task that makes all future layer additions trivial — one new file = one new layer. Files: features/terrain.ts, features/imagery.ts, features/contours.ts, features/labels.ts, features/registry.ts (or similar).
data.title: Architecture refactor: layer registry + features/ directory

data.body: NOTE: RP-16 (data pipeline spike) must run alongside this task — the layer interface design and how each layer loads its own data are tightly coupled. Plan for RP-4 should not finalize the layer init() signature until RP-16's data format decisions are made.
NOTE: implementation committed directly to main (branch checkout was lost during RP-12 juggling). feat/RP-4-layer-registry branch exists but is behind main. Will clean up after review passes.

Review: PASS. All 8 ACs satisfied. main.ts is pure orchestration (43 lines, zero terrain-specific code). types.ts Layer interface correct. terrain.ts owns fetch/CustomHeightmapTerrainProvider/verticalExaggeration, assigns to viewer.terrainProvider post-construction per key decision. registry.ts has register/initAll(Promise.all)/get/getAll, no show/hide on registry per spec. state.ts AppState+defaultState, exaggeration typed as 1|typeof EXAGGERATION_SCALE (correct binary toggle). tsc clean. Breadcrumb: terrain show/hide controls globe.show — fine now (only layer), but future layers that don't own the globe will need a different contract.
